---
title: "Adding NH_label() and NH_describe() to nhanesR — a Claude Code session"
author: "David Winsemius (with Claude Code / Anthropic)"
date: "2026-06-21"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

This document is a lightly edited transcript of the Claude Code session in which
`NH_label()` and `NH_describe()` were designed and added to the **nhanesR**
package. It is intended to give a collaborator a sense of how iterative
AI-assisted development works in practice.

---

## Context

The starting point was the **nhanesR** package
(<https://dwinsemius.github.io/nhanesR/>), which provides tools for downloading
and analyzing NHANES data with mortality linkage. After following the
"Getting Started" example and calling `Hmisc::describe()` on a downloaded
data frame, the output looked like this:

```
LBXTC
       n  missing distinct ...
```

The raw NHANES variable codes (`LBXTC`, `LBDHDL`, `LBXGGT`, …) are opaque to
anyone not already fluent in NHANES conventions. The goal of this session was
to add a function that injects the plain-language CDC description — which
`nhanes_search_variables()` already returns as `variable_desc` — immediately
below each variable name in the printed output.

---

## Turn 1 — Initial request

**User:**

> I just tried following the "getting started" code at
> https://dwinsemius.github.io/nhanesR/. It worked without error. When I then
> looked at it with `Hmisc::describe.data.frame()` I realized that a new user
> who was not already an avid NHANES user might experience the result as a blast
> from a firehose. I would like to add a function that is derived from
> `Hmisc::describe` that prints the text description that would come from the
> value `variable_desc` returned by `nhanes_search_variables`. When the
> `nhanesR::NH_describe` function is `print()`-ed the `variable_desc` value
> should be printed immediately following the variable name. Looking at the
> unexported `print.describe` function with `getAnywhere()` I see that it
> expects the `gt` package to be available. The describe function is quite
> complex but I think we should only copy the data.frame methods and do not see
> a need to include the LaTeX methods for output.

**Claude Code:**

Explored the package source and the Hmisc internals:

```r
# Key discovery: print.describe.single just cats $descript then \n
# So if we append "\n  <description>" to $descript in each element,
# the standard print.describe machinery renders it for free.

function (x, ...) {
    des <- x$descript
    ...
    cat(des, "\n")          # <-- this is where the variable name prints
    print(x$counts, ...)
    ...
}
```

Also found that `describe.data.frame` names its result list by the column
names of the input, so `names(result)[i]` gives the variable name for each
element.

**Implementation chosen:** run `Hmisc::describe()`, then walk the result list
and append `"\n  <CDC description>"` to each `$descript` string for columns
that match the catalog. No custom print method required.

---

## Turn 1 — First implementation

Created `R/describe.R` with two functions:

**`NH_describe(x, descriptions = NULL, ...)`** — exported. Calls
`Hmisc::describe()`, then for each variable looks up the CDC description
and splices it into `$descript`.

**`.nhanes_build_desc_map(descriptions)`** — internal. Accepts a named
character vector, a `nhanes_search_variables()` data frame, or `NULL`
(auto-loads from the five cached per-component RDS files in the nhanesR
cache directory without a network call).

Added `Hmisc` to `Suggests` in `DESCRIPTION`.

Smoke-test output:

```
LBXTC
  Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)
       n  missing distinct     Info     Mean  pMedian      Gmd
       3        0        3        1    198.3    197.5    26.67
...
```

---

## Turn 2 — gt package

**User:**

> I think the `gt` package needs to be in Suggests.

**Claude Code:**

Correct — `print.describe` calls `requireNamespace("gt")` when the `which=`
argument is used (for categorical/continuous split display). Added `gt` to
`Suggests` in `DESCRIPTION`.

---

## Turn 3 — Design question: column attributes

**User:**

> Looks like you kept the class of the output as `'describe'` rather than
> adding a `NH_describe` class. Would it be helpful to have the variable
> description text as an attribute for NHANES data frame columns? (or is that
> how you implemented it?)

**Claude Code:**

The first implementation did *not* use attributes — it modified `$descript`
strings transiently inside the describe object; the original data frame was
untouched.

But Hmisc has a native label convention: `attr(col, "label")` is read by
`describe.vector` and automatically appended to the variable name:

```r
# Inside Hmisc:::describe.vector:
labx <- atx$label
if (length(labx) && labx != descript)
    descript <- paste(descript, ":", labx)
```

Setting labels on the columns means plain `Hmisc::describe()` picks them up
without `NH_describe` at all, and they persist across `Hmisc::summary()`,
`Hmisc::html()`, and every other label-aware Hmisc function.

**Proposed design:**

```r
# Label once — descriptions available everywhere in Hmisc
tc <- NH_label(nhanes_download_analyte("total cholesterol"))
Hmisc::describe(tc)

# Or one-shot describe without modifying the stored object
NH_describe(tc)
```

---

## Turn 4 — Refactor ("Sounds good")

**User:** Sounds good.

**Claude Code:**

Rewrote `R/describe.R` with:

**`NH_label(x, descriptions = NULL)`** — new exported function. Walks the
columns of `x`, looks up each name in the catalog, and sets
`attr(col, "label")` if a match is found. Returns `x` with labels attached.
Does not require Hmisc.

**`NH_describe(x, descriptions = NULL, ...)`** — now a thin one-liner:

```r
NH_describe <- function(x, descriptions = NULL, ...) {
  if (!requireNamespace("Hmisc", quietly = TRUE))
    stop("Install Hmisc: install.packages('Hmisc')", call. = FALSE)
  Hmisc::describe(NH_label(x, descriptions = descriptions), ...)
}
```

**`.nhanes_build_desc_map()`** — shared internal helper, logic unchanged.

Smoke-test confirming labels survive into plain `Hmisc::describe()`:

```
df2

 3  Variables      3  Observations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LBXTC : Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)
       n  missing distinct     Info     Mean  pMedian      Gmd
       3        0        3        1    198.3    197.5    26.67
...
LBDHDL : HDL-cholesterol (mg/dL)
...
RIDAGEYR : Age at Screening Adjudicated - Recode
...
```

---

## Final state of R/describe.R

```r
NH_label <- function(x, descriptions = NULL) {
  desc_map <- .nhanes_build_desc_map(descriptions)
  if (is.null(desc_map)) return(x)
  for (vn in names(x)) {
    if (vn %in% names(desc_map)) {
      lbl <- trimws(desc_map[[vn]])
      if (!is.na(lbl) && nzchar(lbl))
        attr(x[[vn]], "label") <- lbl
    }
  }
  x
}

NH_describe <- function(x, descriptions = NULL, ...) {
  if (!requireNamespace("Hmisc", quietly = TRUE))
    stop("Install Hmisc: install.packages('Hmisc')", call. = FALSE)
  Hmisc::describe(NH_label(x, descriptions = descriptions), ...)
}
```

---

## How to use

```r
library(nhanesR)

# Download an analyte (e.g. total cholesterol across all cycles)
tc <- nhanes_download_analyte("total cholesterol")

# Option A: label once, use anywhere in Hmisc
tc <- NH_label(tc)
Hmisc::describe(tc)
Hmisc::html(Hmisc::describe(tc))   # needs gt

# Option B: one-shot labelled describe
NH_describe(tc)

# Supply your own descriptions (e.g. from a prior search)
vars <- nhanes_search_variables("cholesterol")
NH_describe(tc, descriptions = vars)
```

---

## Key design decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| Labels as `attr(col, "label")` not modified `$descript` | Hmisc reads `"label"` attributes natively; labels persist across all Hmisc functions |
| `NH_label()` separate from `NH_describe()` | Users who want persistent labels don't have to call `NH_describe` repeatedly |
| Hmisc in `Suggests`, not `Imports` | Not all nhanesR users need Hmisc; checked at runtime with `requireNamespace()` |
| `gt` in `Suggests` | `print.describe` calls `requireNamespace("gt")` for the `which=` display modes |
| Catalog loaded from cache, no network | The five per-component RDS files written by `nhanes_search_variables()` are read directly; no HTTP request needed |
| No custom `print` method | The standard `print.describe.single` already renders `"VARNAME : label"` once the label attribute is set |

---

## Follow-up: label preservation through nhanes_stack()

**User:**

> When we harmonise variables with the same meaning but different "common
> names", do the labels get populated meaningfully?

**Claude Code:**

Traced the full pipeline by reading `harmonize.R` and `merge.R`:

1. **Download** — `haven::read_xpt()` populates `attr(col, "label")` from the
   SAS metadata embedded in the XPT file (e.g. `LBXTC` gets label
   `"Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)"`).
2. **Harmonize** — both code paths (`unit=` and `mapping=`) rename via
   `names(df)[idx] <- name`. This changes only the column name; attributes are
   untouched. Labels survive the rename. ✓
3. **Stack** — `nhanes_stack()` uses `do.call(rbind, dfs)`. Base R
   `rbind.data.frame()` silently drops column attributes. Labels lost here. ✗
4. **`NH_label()` on harmonized data** — harmonized names (`TC_mgdl`, etc.) are
   user-defined and absent from the CDC catalog, so the lookup finds nothing. ✗

**Fix: preserve labels in `nhanes_stack()`**

Added seven lines to `R/merge.R` around the `rbind` call:

```r
# Save labels before rbind: base R rbind.data.frame() drops column attributes
saved_labels <- lapply(dfs[[1L]], function(col) attr(col, "label"))

out <- do.call(rbind, dfs)
rownames(out) <- NULL

for (nm in names(saved_labels)) {
  lbl <- saved_labels[[nm]]
  if (!is.null(lbl) && nm %in% names(out))
    attr(out[[nm]], "label") <- lbl
}
```

Labels are read from the first frame (they are consistent across cycles after
harmonization) and restored after `rbind`. The full pipeline now works end to
end:

```
download → harmonize → stack → Hmisc::describe()
                                  shows "TC_mgdl : Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)"
```

Smoke-test output after the fix:

```
TC_mgdl : Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)
       n  missing distinct     Info     Mean  pMedian      Gmd
       4        0        4        1    201.2    201.2     22.5
...
HDL_mgdl : HDL-cholesterol (mg/dL)
...
```

`NH_label()` remains useful for raw per-cycle frames that haven't passed
through `nhanes_harmonize()`, or for variables whose XPT labels are absent.

---

## Follow-up: replicate weight suppression in NH_describe()

**User:**

> There are a lot of replicate weights. [observed during live test on the
> getting-started example — stacked DEMO across 10 cycles]

**Claude Code:**

Investigated which file was causing this by downloading and inspecting the
stacked DEMO frame. Confirmed the source:

```
WTMREP01 … WTMREP52   # 52 MEC exam balanced repeated replication weights
WTIREP01 … WTIREP52   # 52 interview BRR weights
```

108 columns in total, present because one or more recent NHANES DEMO cycles
bundle BRR replicate weights alongside the primary weights. These are an
alternative variance estimation approach (balanced repeated replication) — not
needed when using Taylor-series linearization via `svydesign()`, which is the
standard nhanesR / NHANES analytic approach.

**User:**

> Can we suppress printing those by default and allow them to show up when a
> particular option is chosen, perhaps `all_weights`?

**Fix: `all_weights = FALSE` default in `NH_describe()`**

Added an `all_weights` argument. Before passing to `Hmisc::describe()`, columns
whose names match `REP\d+$` are dropped unless `all_weights = TRUE`. An
informative message reports what was hidden:

```r
if (!all_weights) {
  drop <- grep("REP\\d+$", names(x), ignore.case = TRUE)
  if (length(drop)) {
    cli::cli_inform(
      "Hiding {length(drop)} replicate weight column{?s} \\
       ({.val {names(x)[drop[1]]}} – {.val {names(x)[drop[length(drop)]]}}).  \\
       Use {.code all_weights = TRUE} to include them."
    )
    x <- x[, -drop, drop = FALSE]
  }
}
```

Live output with the default:

```
Hiding 104 replicate weight columns ("WTMREP01" – "WTIREP52").
Use `all_weights = TRUE` to include them.
```

Usage:

```r
# Default — replicate weights hidden
NH_describe(demo)

# Show everything
NH_describe(demo, all_weights = TRUE)
```
